summary: Completed testing of customer auth page with Google OAuth integration. Backend: 13/13 tests passed. Frontend: 12/14 tests passed. Found 1 bug: registration success screen with generated password is not shown due to immediate redirect.

ui_bugs.issue: Registration success screen with generated password is not displayed - user is immediately redirected to /menu-catalog after registration because the `if (customer)` check triggers before the success state can be rendered
ui_bugs.root_cause: In CustomerContext.register(), fetchMe() is called which sets the customer state. The CustomerAuth component has `if (customer) { navigate('/menu-catalog'); return null; }` at the top, which triggers before the success message with generated password can be displayed.
ui_bugs.suggested_fix: Add a local state like `showPasswordScreen` that prevents the redirect until user clicks 'Start Ordering', or modify the register function to not call fetchMe() immediately

issues: React warning: 'Cannot update a component while rendering a different component' - setState called in render for BrowserRouter/CustomerAuth

passed_tests: Backend: Health check endpoint returns 200
Backend: Customer registration creates account and returns password
Backend: Duplicate email registration returns 400
Backend: Missing fields registration returns 422
Backend: Customer login with valid credentials returns 200
Backend: Customer login with invalid password returns 401
Backend: Customer login with non-existent email returns 401
Backend: POST /api/customer/auth/google-session endpoint exists
Backend: Google session endpoint requires session_id (returns 400)
Backend: Google session endpoint rejects invalid session_id (returns 401)
Backend: Google session endpoint rejects empty session_id
Backend: /api/customer/me without token returns 401
Backend: /api/customer/me with invalid token returns 401
Frontend: Customer auth page renders at /customer-auth with Apple design
Frontend: 'Welcome back.' heading displayed with Outfit font styling
Frontend: Google 'Continue with Google' button visible with colored Google logo SVG
Frontend: Login/Register pill toggle switches between modes
Frontend: Login mode shows email + password fields and 'Sign In' button
Frontend: Register mode shows name + email + phone fields and 'Create Account' button
Frontend: Login with invalid credentials shows error message
Frontend: Mobile responsiveness (375px) - all elements visible, no horizontal overflow
Frontend: Google OAuth callback route works - hash with session_id shows GoogleAuthCallback component
Frontend: GoogleAuthCallback processes invalid session and redirects with error message
Frontend: Error passed via location.state displays on page load (for failed Google auth)
failed_tests: Frontend: Register flow shows generated password - FAILED (user redirected before password shown)
action_items: FIX: CustomerAuth component should show generated password after registration before redirecting. The `if (customer)` redirect check should be bypassed when `generatedPassword` state is set.
MINOR: Fix React warning about setState during render in CustomerAuth component
critical_code_review_comments: CustomerAuth line 31-34: The immediate redirect when `customer` is set prevents the registration success screen from being displayed. Consider adding `&& !generatedPassword` to the condition.
CustomerContext.register(): Calling fetchMe() immediately after registration sets the customer state, triggering the redirect before the password can be shown to the user.

test_credentials.test_customers: Created during tests with auto-generated passwords

context_for_next_testing_agent: Customer auth page with Google OAuth tested. All backend endpoints working correctly. Google OAuth flow: (1) Click 'Continue with Google' → redirects to auth.emergentagent.com, (2) After Google auth, user returns to /customer-auth#session_id=xxx, (3) Routes.jsx detects session_id in hash and renders GoogleAuthCallback, (4) GoogleAuthCallback calls POST /api/customer/auth/google-session, (5) Backend validates session with Emergent Auth API. One bug found: registration success screen with generated password is not shown due to immediate redirect when customer state is set. The fix is simple: add `&& !generatedPassword` to the redirect condition in CustomerAuth.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: Race condition between setting success state and customer state redirect
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Change line 31 in CustomerAuth from `if (customer)` to `if (customer && !generatedPassword)` to allow the password screen to be displayed
